title: Rewrite engine as wave loop
description:
Replace the ralph iteration loop and mode dispatch in internal/engine/engine.go with the new wave-based loop.

New engine flow:
1. Compute waves via wave.ComputeWaves()
2. Execute first wave via runWave()
3. Handle signals (park awaiting tasks)
4. Update budget tracker
5. Re-compute waves (completed tasks unblock next wave, approved tasks re-enter)
6. If no waves and tasks remain: enter watch mode (fsnotify on .tick/issues/) or exit
7. Loop until all tasks complete, budget exceeded, or context cancelled

Remove:
- Ralph single-task iteration loop (getNextTask -> runIteration -> repeat)
- Mode dispatch (UseWorktree conditionals, pool/swarm branching)
- Stuck-loop detection (waves handle ordering naturally)
- Debounce logic (waves are discrete, not continuous)

Keep:
- Budget tracking (tokens, cost, iterations)
- Signal handling (ParseSignals, handleSignal, SetAwaiting)
- Watch mode (fsnotify wait for human verdicts)
- RunResult structure
- Callbacks (OnIterationEnd, OnSignal, OnAgentState) for board integration
- Epic context generation
acceptance_criteria: Engine runs wave loop. Single code path (no mode branching). Budget tracking works. Signal handling parks tasks correctly. Watch mode detects human verdicts. All engine tests updated and passing.
closed_reason: Engine rewritten as wave loop. Ralph iteration loop replaced with wave.Compute + wave.Runner.RunWave pattern. Removed checkpoint, debounce, UseWorktree, RepoRoot from engine. Wave runner decoupled from engine (no circular import). Engine prompt builder injected into wave runner via PromptFunc.
